question_text: Two machines perform the same task. Machine A uses 500 J of energy and produces 400 J of useful work. Machine B uses 300 J of energy and produces 210 J of useful work. Which machine is more efficient, and by what percentage?
correct_answer: Machine B is more efficient. Machine A: 80% efficiency, Machine B: 70% efficiency. Wait—Machine A is more efficient by 10 percentage points.
explanation: Machine A efficiency = (400 ÷ 500) × 100 = 80%. Machine B efficiency = (210 ÷ 300) × 100 = 70%. Machine A is more efficient despite using more energy overall because a higher percentage of its input energy becomes useful work.
